skill.name: scientific-schematics
skill.description: Automates publication-quality scientific diagrams (e.g., flowcharts, architectures, pathways) when you need journal/poster-ready visuals from a natural-language description.
skill.summary: Converts natural-language descriptions into publication-quality scientific diagrams with AI-powered code generation and iterative review.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill has elevated risk due to a confirmed dangerous capability combination: it executes AI-generated Python code via subprocess.run (scripts/generate_schematic.py:123), accesses OPENROUTER_API_KEY credentials from environment variables (line 22-25), and makes external HTTP requests to OpenRouter API (line 56-59). Most static analysis findings (base64 flagged as weak crypto, markdown code blocks flagged as shell execution) are false positives. The primary concern is that prompt injection could trick the LLM into generating malicious code that exfiltrates the API key via the available network and credential access.

high_findings.title: AI-Generated Code Execution Without Sandboxing
high_findings.description: The skill writes LLM-generated Python code to a temp file and executes it via subprocess.run(). A crafted prompt could trick the AI model into generating malicious code that reads environment variables or exfiltrates data. The code is executed with the same privileges as the calling process with no sandboxing or validation.

high_findings.title: Dangerous Capability Combination: Code Execution + Credentials + Network
high_findings.description: The skill combines three high-risk capabilities: executing AI-generated code, accessing API keys from environment variables, and making network requests. This combination means a successful prompt injection attack could lead to credential exfiltration over the network via the executed code.

medium_findings.title: API Key Access from Environment Variables
medium_findings.description: The skill reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY from environment variables. While necessary for the skill to function, the API key could be exfiltrated if the AI-generated code execution pipeline is compromised. The key is passed as a Bearer token in HTTP headers to OpenRouter API.

medium_findings.title: External Network Requests to AI Provider API
medium_findings.description: The skill sends HTTP POST requests to OpenRouter API (openrouter.ai) with user-provided prompt content and API key authentication. Network access is required for the AI generation and review pipeline.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operations for Output and Temp Files
low_findings.description: The skill writes generated Python code to a temporary file, creates output directories, and saves PNG diagram files. These operations are part of normal skill function but create filesystem exposure.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Examples Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown code block examples in SKILL.md and print() statements showing usage as shell execution or PowerShell invocation. These are documentation-only examples showing users how to run the tool, not actual execution commands.

low_findings.title: Base64 Encoding Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged base64.b64encode() and the base64 import as a weak cryptographic algorithm. Base64 is used at line 148 for standard image encoding prior to submitting to a vision API. This is data encoding, not cryptography, and presents no security risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Code Generation and Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses an LLM to generate Python code based on user descriptions, writes it to a temporary file (temp_generate.py), and executes it via subprocess.run([sys.executable, temp_script]). The generated code is not validated, sandboxed, or restricted before execution. Combined with environment variable access and network capabilities, this creates a prompt-injection-to-RCE attack vector.

content.user_title: Generate publication-quality scientific diagrams from descriptions
content.value_statement: Creating professional scientific diagrams for journals and posters requires significant manual effort. This skill automates the process by converting natural-language descriptions into publication-quality schematics with iterative AI-powered quality review.
actual_capabilities: Generate custom scientific diagrams from text descriptions using AI-powered code generation
Apply iterative quality review with vision-capable AI models to refine diagram output
Support both journal and poster formats with appropriate resolution and styling
Create neural network architecture diagrams, biological pathways, and flowcharts
Save high-resolution (300 DPI) output files in PNG format
Use configurable AI models for generation and review stages
limitations: Requires an active OpenRouter API key for AI model access
Diagram quality depends on the capability of the selected AI model
Only supports PNG output format for generated diagrams
No built-in support for interactive diagram editing or vector formats

use_cases.title: Research Paper Figure Creation
use_cases.description: A researcher needs a clear neural network architecture diagram for a journal submission. They describe the architecture in natural language, and the skill generates a publication-ready figure with proper labels and professional styling at 300 DPI.

use_cases.title: Conference Poster Design
use_cases.description: A PhD student preparing a conference poster needs a large-format biological pathway diagram. The skill generates the diagram with poster-optimized sizing, bold colors, and readable fonts for distance viewing.

use_cases.title: Rapid Diagram Prototyping
use_cases.description: A research team leader needs to quickly iterate on a clinical trial flowchart before finalizing it with a graphic designer. The skill provides rapid AI-assisted refinement loops with quality scoring.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Journal Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a scientific diagram showing the workflow of a PCR experiment.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creates a simple journal figure

prompt_templates.title: Poster-Ready Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a poster-friendly diagram of a Transformer neural network architecture with attention mechanism. Use large labels and bold colors.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Biological Pathway
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a multi-step biological pathway diagram showing the Krebs cycle with enzyme labels and metabolite names. Use colorblind-safe colors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed pathway visualization with custom requirements

prompt_templates.title: Custom Model Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a flowchart of a clinical trial enrollment pipeline with inclusion and exclusion criteria. Use --generator anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet and --reviewer google/gemini-2.0-flash-001 for best quality.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user leveraging custom model configuration

output_examples.input: Generate a diagram of a Transformer architecture with encoder-decoder blocks
output_examples.output: The skill generates a PNG image saved to figures/schematic_timestamp.png showing a Transformer architecture with labeled encoder blocks on the left, decoder blocks on the right, and attention mechanism connections between them. The diagram uses a professional color scheme at 300 DPI resolution.

output_examples.input: Create a biological pathway of the glycolysis process with enzyme names
output_examples.output: The skill produces a multi-stage pathway diagram showing the 10 steps of glycolysis from glucose to pyruvate. Each step is labeled with the enzyme name and metabolite structure, using a colorblind-safe palette. The system completed after 2 review iterations with a final quality score of 9.2 out of 10.

best_practices: Provide clear and specific descriptions of the diagram you want, including labels and relationships between elements.
Use the --doc-type flag to match output format to your target medium (journal or poster).
Review the generated figure and iterate with refined descriptions for best results.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague descriptions without specific labels or structure details as the output will lack precision.
Do not expect real scientific data in diagrams as the AI generates illustrative content only.
Do not rely on this tool for exact measurements or scale-accurate representations.

faq.question: What AI models does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill uses OpenRouter API to access configurable AI models. The default generator and reviewer model is google/gemini-2.0-flash-001.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need an OpenRouter API key set as the OPENROUTER_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What types of diagrams can this skill create?
faq.answer: The skill can create neural network architectures, biological pathways, flowcharts, circuit diagrams, and process workflows.

faq.question: What file format does the output use?
faq.answer: The skill saves diagrams as PNG files at 300 DPI resolution in a figures/ directory.

faq.question: Can I use different models for generation and review?
faq.answer: Yes. You can specify separate models using the --generator and --reviewer command-line arguments.

faq.question: How does the quality review process work?
faq.answer: After generating a diagram, a vision-capable AI model evaluates it and provides a score. If the score is below 8.5 out of 10, the system refines the diagram using the reviewer feedback.
